epigraph.text: A cord of three strands is not quickly broken.

content.text:
The triangle is the simplest polygonג€”a closed figure with straight 
sides. With just three sides and three angles, triangles are 
remarkably strong and appear everywhere from pyramids to roof trusses 
to musical instruments.

content.title: Triangle
content.content:
A **triangle** is a closed figure with three 
straight sides and three angles. The sum of the three angles in any 
triangle is always $180ֲ°$.

content.text:
We name triangles by their vertices. Triangle $ABC$ (written $\triangle ABC$) 
has:

content.title: Triangle Angle Sum
content.content:
The sum of the interior angles of any triangle is $180ֲ°$:
\[
\angle A + \angle B + \angle C = 180ֲ°
\]
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Finding a missing angle
content.problem: A triangle has angles measuring $45ֲ°$ and $70ֲ°$. Find the third angle.
content.solution:
Let the third angle be $x$.
$$
45 + 70 + x &= 180 \\
115 + x &= 180 \\
x &= 65
$$
The third angle measures $65ֲ°$.

content.content:
The Trinityג€”Father, Son, and Holy Spiritג€”is often symbolized by 
a triangle. Just as the three angles of a triangle form one complete 
whole ($180ֲ°$), the three persons of the Trinity are one God.

content.title: Types by Angles
content.content:
- **Acute triangle**: All three angles are less than $90ֲ°$

- **Right triangle**: One angle is exactly $90ֲ°$

- **Obtuse triangle**: One angle is greater than $90ֲ°$

content.title: Classifying by angles
content.solution:
1. All angles are less than $90ֲ°$ ג†’ **acute**

2. One angle is $90ֲ°$ ג†’ **right**

3. One angle ($120ֲ°$) is greater than $90ֲ°$ ג†’ **obtuse**

content.title: Types by Sides
content.content:
- **Equilateral triangle**: All three sides are equal

- **Isosceles triangle**: At least two sides are equal

- **Scalene triangle**: No sides are equal

content.text: Important fact: In an equilateral triangle, all three angles are $60ֲ°$.

content.title: Classifying by sides
content.problem:
Classify each triangle by its sides:

1. Sides: 5 cm, 5 cm, 5 cm

2. Sides: 7 cm, 7 cm, 4 cm

3. Sides: 3 cm, 4 cm, 5 cm
content.solution:
1. All sides equal ג†’ **equilateral**

2. Two sides equal ג†’ **isosceles**

3. No sides equal ג†’ **scalene**

content.title: Scripture
content.content:
The equilateral triangle, with its perfect symmetry, has been used 
as a symbol of the Trinity throughout Christian history. Each side 
is equal, just as each person of the Trinity is fully God.

content.title: Algebraic angle problem
content.problem:
The angles of a triangle measure $x$, $(x + 30)ֲ°$, and $(2x - 10)ֲ°$. 
Find all three angles.

marginNotes.text:
For where two or three gather in my 
name, there am I with them.

God saw all that he had made, and it 
was very good.

For the Lord gives wisdom; from his 
mouth come knowledge and understanding.

marginNotes.author: Euclid
marginNotes.text:
The laws of nature are but the mathematical 
thoughts of God.

marginNotes.author: Pythagoras
marginNotes.text: There is geometry in the humming of the strings.
